allTimeDividendsEarnedTooltip.body: Your total dividend payment is $422,553.66. This is how much in dividends you've received so far. Dividends are payments companies make to pass income to their investors.

Our software uses dividends to help keep your tax bill low.

We use dividends to rebalance your portfolio. Over time, your portfolio's asset allocation will drift as some asset classes outperform others. When you receive dividends, our software reinvests them in underweight asset classes, moving you closer to your target allocation with fewer tax consequences.

Buying additional shares also means you'll have more tax lots in your portfolio. This translates into more opportunities for our software to conduct tax-loss harvesting.
Share with a friend, and you could both get an additional $5,000 managed for free.

allTimeReturnsTooltip.body:
This is how much your portfolio has grown since your first investment. 
Time-weighted return is a good measure of the performance of portfolio allocation, since it removes the distorting effects of the timing of your deposits and withdrawals.

This is the average return on the deposits you've made since your first investment, taking your portfolio's performance and the timing of your deposits and withdrawals into consideration. 
This is a good measure of your returns based on the timing of your deposits (though we advise against timing the market). For example, if you made a large deposit right before the market rose, your average money-weighted return would increase.
Share with a friend, and you could both get an additional $5,000 managed for free.

allTimeTaxSavingsTooltip.body: This is your estimated tax savings from our Tax-Loss Harvesting service. We don't factor in any capital gains you may have realized in external taxable investment accounts, but if you did realize gains in external accounts, our service could lower your tax bill even further.

Our software harvested $621,348.69 in tax losses. Those losses can be used to offset $17,810.03 of the short-term gains and $19,815.15 of the long-term gains you have at Wealthfront. You may be able to use any remaining losses to reduce your taxable income.

Based on your reported location and income, we assumed you would pay 32% federal income tax, 9.30% state income tax, and a 15% tax rate on your long-term capital gains. This means you could save an estimated $25,532.12 in taxes.

allTimeDividendsEarnedTooltip.body: Your total dividend payment is $422,553.66. This is how much in dividends you've received so far. Dividends are payments companies make to pass income to their investors.

Our software uses dividends to help keep your tax bill low.

We use dividends to rebalance your portfolio. Over time, your portfolio's asset allocation will drift as some asset classes outperform others. When you receive dividends, our software reinvests them in underweight asset classes, moving you closer to your target allocation with fewer tax consequences.

Buying additional shares also means you'll have more tax lots in your portfolio. This translates into more opportunities for our software to conduct tax-loss harvesting.
Share with a friend, and you could both get an additional $5,000 managed for free.

allTimeReturnsTooltip.body:
This is how much your portfolio has grown since your first investment. 
Time-weighted return is a good measure of the performance of portfolio allocation, since it removes the distorting effects of the timing of your deposits and withdrawals.

This is the average return on the deposits you've made since your first investment, taking your portfolio's performance and the timing of your deposits and withdrawals into consideration. 
This is a good measure of your returns based on the timing of your deposits (though we advise against timing the market). For example, if you made a large deposit right before the market rose, your average money-weighted return would increase.
Share with a friend, and you could both get an additional $5,000 managed for free.
